summary: Resolve staged SSOT drift across platform/runtime ownership, checker parity, supporting docs, and the cross-platform runtime-linker path bug. Verification is complete, but the release gate remains on hold because the repo-wide strict safety baseline is still red outside this remediation slice.
invariants: INV-D-1: Context injection and bugfix discovery guidance must resolve through agents-manifest.yaml without parallel operator-facing routing drift.
INV-OBS-1: Governance checker outcomes must be consistent across Python and PowerShell for council summaries, manifest requirements, project-doc README references, and traceability_refs validation.
INV-D-2: README.md section 'Checks' remains the single verification-command SSOT; supporting docs and templates must point to it rather than duplicate command lists.
INV-LIF-1: Runtime projection docs must reference canonical owners rather than restating projected runtime-path or config-content facts.
INV-EDGE-1: Final closure requires both strict safety evidence and a deterministic runtime-linker failure-path witness.

witnesses.signal: Manifest and supporting docs align on repo discovery and bugfix inject behavior
witnesses.record_location: agents-manifest.yaml, docs/agents/agents_index.md, and python3 scripts/check_governance_core.py stdout
witnesses.pass_criteria: default_inject includes docs/agents/10-repo-discovery.md, bugfix routing is manifest-referential, and governance core checks pass

witnesses.signal: Python and PowerShell governance checkers accept the same current tree
witnesses.record_location: python3 scripts/check_governance_core.py stdout; pwsh scripts/check_agents_manifest.ps1 stdout; pwsh scripts/check_project_docs.ps1 stdout; pwsh scripts/check_change_records.ps1 stdout
witnesses.pass_criteria: all four commands exit 0 on the remediated tree

witnesses.signal: Supporting docs redirect verification commands to README instead of enumerating their own lists
witnesses.pass_criteria: duplicate command lists are removed or replaced with explicit README 'Checks' pointers

witnesses.signal: Runtime/status docs point to canonical owners and runtime decision registry
witnesses.pass_criteria: runtime facts are referential and the runtime projection authority decision is present

witnesses.signal: Strict safety and disposable runtime-linker witnesses are captured explicitly
witnesses.record_location: python3 scripts/check_governance_core.py --fail-on-safety-warnings stdout plus disposable 2026-03-29 linker runs for `-Include skills` and `-Include subagents -IncludeCompatibility`
witnesses.pass_criteria: portable skills projections create `../../docs/...` links on POSIX, the `.cursor/agents` conflict path fails closed, and any remaining strict-safety blockers are explicitly recorded with the release gate held

residual_risk: The release gate remains on hold because strict safety checks still fail on pre-existing X-Bookmarks Import scripts. Runtime-linker witnesses now pass, but file hard-link validation in docs/agents/link_repo_assets.ps1 still treats same-content plain files as equivalent to links, and TOML validation is skipped in this environment because neither tomllib nor tomli is available.

reviewers.scope: Codex config ownership, runtime decision record, linker fail-closed behavior
Python/PowerShell council-summary, manifest, project-doc, and traceability validation parity
manifest routing, checks SSOT, template/runbook duplication, runtime-path literal cleanup
strict safety gate, failure-path witness, and release readiness
final linker fix coherence, witness alignment, and residual risk check

findings.issue: Strict safety baseline remains red because of pre-existing X-Bookmarks Import findings outside this remediation slice.
findings.recommendation: Keep release gate on hold until those repo-wide safety violations are triaged or explicitly accepted.

findings.issue: File hard-link validation remains content-based, so a same-content plain file can still be treated as an acceptable link outcome.
findings.recommendation: Tighten file-link validation in a follow-up change so hard-link acceptance uses filesystem identity rather than content hash equality.

council_summary.conflicts: One SSOT reviewer proposed broadening scripts/setup_repo_platform_assets.ps1 to aggregate per-asset failures. Deferred because the verified root cause and single-authority fix point were in docs/agents/link_repo_assets.ps1, and wrapper changes were not required to restore deterministic witnesses.
council_summary.reconciliation_decision: accepted the staged fixes for Stages 0-3 plus the linker authority fix for cross-platform relative-path handling, broken-link reporting, and missing-source cleanup logging; deferred wrapper aggregation, helper deduplication, and hard-link identity validation
council_summary.residual_risks: The strict safety gate is still failing outside the remediated files; file hard-link validation remains content-based; TOML validation is skipped in this environment because neither tomllib nor tomli is available.
verification_links: bash disposable witness: pwsh -File docs/agents/link_repo_assets.ps1 -Include skills -Force -RepoRoot <temp-repo>
bash disposable witness: pwsh -File docs/agents/link_repo_assets.ps1 -Include subagents -IncludeCompatibility -RepoRoot <temp-repo>

validation_context.evidence_plan: Keep baseline governance checks green, capture strict-safety failure output, verify portable relative-skill links, and verify the `.cursor/agents` fail-closed conflict witness in a disposable repo.
